content:
# Microfacies

**Summary**: Small-scale geological units characterized by specific mineralogy, texture, and sedimentary structures.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2024-allam|Allam, 2024]] identified five distinct microfacies to characterize the carbonate reservoir.

- [[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024]] analyzed microfacies to characterize reservoir texture and properties.

- [[1989-abdou|Abdou, 1989]] performed microfacies analysis to interpret the depositional environment.

## Scientific Context

- [[2024-allam|Allam, 2024]] Microfacies are critical for understanding the heterogeneity of carbonate reservoirs.

- [[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024]] analyzed microfacies to characterize reservoir texture and properties.

- [[1989-abdou|Abdou, 1989]] performed microfacies analysis to interpret the depositional environment.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2024-allam|Allam, 2024]] identified through thin section analysis and SEM imaging

## Key Findings Across Sources

- MF-1 (Calcareous mudstone) acted as a permeability barrier due to its lack of pore space. ([[2024-allam|Allam, 2024, p. 30]])

- Ten carbonate microfacies were identified, ranging from mudstone to grainstone. ([[2024-hassan|Hassan, 2024, p. 14]])

- 10 successive microfacies were identified in the Umm Gudair#1 well. ([[1989-abdou|Abdou, 1989, p. 16]])
